articles.title: Japan consumer sentiment plunges in March as US-Iran conflict weighs
articles.expand: The main consumer confidence index takes a dive from 39.7 in February to 33.3 in March. And that is the weakest reading since the middle of last year. The US-Iran conflict is threatening to derail the consumer recovery in Japan, not least with higher petrol prices and spillovers to higher inflation. And for the BOJ, it isn't the good kind of inflat...

articles.title: USD/JPY Price Forecast: Remains below 159.00 as bulls stay cautious amid softer USD demand
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair fails just ahead of the 159.00 mark during the Asian session on Thursday, stalling the previous day's modest recovery from sub-158.00 levels or a nearly three-week low.

articles.title: Gold holds above $4,700 as geopolitical risks counter dovish Fed as bearish bias persists
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) remains on the back foot through the Asian session on Thursday, though it manages to hold above the $4,700 mark and, for now, seems to have stalled the previous day's pullback from a three-week high.

articles.expand: Bank of Japan (BoJ) Governor Kazuo Ueda said that real interest rates are clearly negative and keeping the country's financial conditions accommodative, Reuters reported on Thursday.

articles.title: RBNZ’s Breman: New Zealand could see stronger growth if Middle East conflict ends soon
articles.expand: Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) Governor Anna Breman said that domestic economy could see stronger growth this year if there was a swift resolution to the conflict in the Middle East, Reuters reported on Thursday. She added that the previous rate cuts were still providing some stimulus.
